expectations.text: Correctly identifies DSP-73 as the appropriate temporary export license form
expectations.evidence: Section 'Required License: DSP-73 (Temporary Export)' clearly identifies DSP-73 under 22 CFR § 123.5 as the correct instrument because the item is leaving temporarily and will return.

expectations.text: Confirms USML Category XII for night-vision/optical targeting equipment
expectations.evidence: Opening section 'Classification Confirmation: USML Category XII' explicitly confirms Category XII — Fire Control, Range Finder, Optical and Guidance and Control Equipment with no ambiguity.

expectations.text: Explains the D-Trade portal application process
expectations.evidence: Section 'Application Process — D-Trade Portal' explains submission via dtrade.pmddtc.state.gov with all required fields and 30-60 day processing time.

expectations.text: Notes key conditions: items cannot be transferred to foreign ownership and must return by license expiry
expectations.evidence: Key License Conditions section explicitly covers 'No Transfer to Foreign Ownership' and 'Items Must Return by License Expiry' as numbered conditions.

expectations.text: Mentions that re-export from UK to other European countries during the show circuit may require DDTC approval
expectations.evidence: Section 'Re-Export Across European Countries — DDTC Approval Required' states 'Re-export of ITAR-controlled defense articles from one foreign country to another requires prior DDTC approval (22 CFR § 126.14)' and offers two options.
